metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3650
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Absence of Bai3 reduces CF-EPSCs what can be rescued lentivirus injection of WT Bai3 (Fig. 4); and C1qrl1 interaction with Bai3 is essential for the maintenance of CF synapse in adults (Fig. 5)

28/5/2018 Pim
- Authors show that:
1) C1ql1-Bai3 Triggers Pruning of Surplus CFs in Adult Mice
2) The C1ql1-Bai3 Interaction Is Required to Maintain CF Synapses
And state: "These results indicate that C1ql1 has dual functions: strengthening existing CF synapses and inducing the elimination of inactive synapses". Experimental description: Here, we show that C1ql1, a member of the C1q family of proteins, is provided by climbing fibers (CFs) and serves as a crucial anterograde signal to determine and maintain the single-winner CF in the mouse cerebellum throughout development and adulthood. Abstract, p. 316
